sentence_reviews.text: Formal model verification proves that a model satisfies a specified property over an input region or abstraction, such as robustness, bounded output, or monotonicity.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Seshia, Sadigh & Sastry survey formal methods for ML, including formal specifications and verification of neural-network properties such as robustness and safety over input sets/abstractions.
sentence_reviews.notes: Directly supported in substance by the formal methods survey.

sentence_reviews.text: It can replace sampled evidence with mathematical guarantees for narrowly defined high-consequence properties.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The survey contrasts formal guarantees/specification satisfaction with empirical testing for ML safety and robustness properties.
sentence_reviews.notes: Mathematical guarantees are supported; high-consequence wording is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The survey covers verification/certification of robustness properties against perturbations.
Formal ML verification includes proving output constraints/bounds for neural networks under specified input regions.
The survey discusses formal methods for ML in control/CPS settings, including safety verification of learned components/controllers.
sentence_reviews.notes: Supported by survey scope, but exact phrase is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: Verification scales poorly with model size and nonlinear complexity, and the specified property may omit real hazards.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The survey discusses scalability challenges and the dependence of formal guarantees on the chosen specification/property.

sentence_reviews.text: Formal verification of models measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Formal methods, specifications & proof obligations.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Formal verification is grounded in formal specifications/properties and proof or solver obligations.

sentence_reviews.text: Formal verification of models checks candidate behavior or artifacts against evidence or constraints from Constraint satisfaction, SAT & SMT solving.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The formal ML survey discusses solver-based verification including SMT/MILP-style encodings; AIMA supports constraint-solving foundations.
sentence_reviews.notes: Core solver relationship is well supported.

sentence_reviews.text: Formal verification of models measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Robustness, distribution shift & OOD evaluation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Formal ML survey covers robustness verification; NIST supports robustness evaluation/governance. OOD is less directly established.
sentence_reviews.notes: Robustness is supported; distribution shift/OOD as an edge dependency is broader.

sentence_reviews.text: Formal verification of models measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Ablation, interventions & causal analysis.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Formal verification can analyze properties under perturbations/interventions, but the packet lacks direct support for causal ablation as a dependency.

edge_reviews.notes: Formal specifications are core to formal model verification.
Solver-backed constraint checking is a core verification method.
Robustness verification is supported, but OOD/distribution-shift dependency is not directly established.
Intervention-style reasoning can be relevant, but causal ablation is not clearly supported by the cited source set.
